nodes.body: Nina and Theo are best friends who love adventure. One sunny afternoon, they explore Grandpa's big backyard and find something special under the old oak tree. It is a dusty jar with a rolled-up map inside. The map is hand-drawn with a dotted line that twists and turns. A red X marks the very end. Nina grins wide and Theo grins too. Which landmark should they follow first? They look at each other, ready for their adventure.

choices.label: Follow the map to the great old oak.
Follow the map to the garden shed.
Follow the map down to the stream.

nodes.body: The dotted line points to the big oak tree at the top of the garden. Nina holds the map while Theo counts their paces carefully. One, two, three, four. The oak is huge and very big, so big that three kids could hide behind it. They get closer and closer and the line starts to split near the roots. It makes two faint trails that go different ways.

choices.label: Search around the roots of the oak.

nodes.body: At the foot of the oak the line splits into two paths. One trail wiggles. It curves toward a mossy hollow log. Something croaks softly from inside. The other trail loops around. It goes to an old tire swing. The swing hangs from a low branch. Ribbons flutter on the rope. Which way does the map mean to go? Nina and Theo look at the map. They point and decide. Let's go this way, they say.

choices.label: Take the trail to the hollow log.
Take the trail to the tire swing.

nodes.body: They follow the trail to the hollow log. It sits in long grass. The log is huge. It is bigger than a barrel. One end is dark. It is cool and shady there. A friendly croaking sound comes from deep inside. It does not sound scary. It sounds warm and happy. It sounds like tiny friends. Nina and Theo step closer. The sound gets louder.

Nina shines her flashlight into the log. The light goes far inside. Theo checks the map. The dotted line goes right through the log. It points out the other side. The croaking grows busier. The sound gets louder. It sounds like a tiny cheerful crowd. Many little voices croak and call. Nina leans in closer. She wants to see more.

choices.label: Follow the map through the log.

nodes.body: Past the log they see a wet mark on the map from an old raindrop. Two little paths lead away now, and they look different from each other. One path is dry and dusty and goes back toward the fence. The other path is soft and mossy and goes down to the damp corner. Which path is the right one? Theo taps the map and asks which way. Nina points and says I think this way is correct.

The dry path curves back and ends at old flowerpots, just pots and nothing more. There is no X here and no treasure at all in this spot. The friends look at each other and smile with a laugh. What a silly trick, they say together. They roll up the map and run back to the porch. They skip and run fast because a big cold glass of lemonade waits for them. Sweet and cold and perfect. They will try again tomorrow for sure.

They follow the mossy path. It is soft under their feet. So soft. It feels springy when they step. Ferns brush their knees. The leaves tickle. The croaking grows louder. It sounds happy and cheerful. Theo whispers softly. Listen, he says. It sounds like hundreds of tiny voices. All talking at once. The friends keep walking. They go deeper and deeper.

The path dips down and they walk into a shady green hollow below them. It is cool and the ground is soft with smooth flat stones dotting the earth. Each stone looks like a tiny doorway or perhaps a little home for someone small. The friends step with care and follow the dotted line deeper into the shade that surrounds them.

choices.label: Step into the shady green hollow.

nodes.body: In the hollow they find a ring. It is made of round pebbles. Like a tiny wall. Behind it sit toads. Fat spotted toads. They are big and plump. They sit on leaf-thrones. Lily-pad chairs. The toads do not hop away. They blink slowly. They look at the visitors. Welcome, the toads seem to say.

choices.label: Tiptoe up to the ring of pebbles.

nodes.body: The biggest toad wears a crown made from clover leaves that are green and fresh. The toad blinks slowly and it looks wise and ancient and powerful. Nina and Theo kneel down very gently so they will not frighten anyone here. And there in the corner the map's red X is drawn right where the king sits.

choices.label: Kneel down by the toad king.

nodes.body: The friends have found a tiny toad kingdom. A whole kingdom in the hollow. A clover-crowned king rules from his throne. His leafy throne. Nina and Theo promise to keep it safe. They promise to keep it secret. They leave a soft green leaf as a gift. A special gift. All the way home they grin and grin. They can hardly stop grinning.

They follow the loop trail to the tire swing that hangs from a low oak branch. The tire is old and weathered and it turns slowly in the gentle breeze. Back and forth and back and forth it moves. Someone has tied bright ribbons to the rope. The ribbons are so pretty and they flutter and dance in the wind. They shine in the sun with yellow and blue and red colors.

choices.label: Look at the ribbons on the swing.

nodes.body: Theo gives the tire a soft push, a gentle push from his hand. He hears a tinkling sound that comes from high up in the tree above. Ting ting ting is the sound. Nina looks at the map and sees the dotted line climbs the trunk. It goes up and up through the branches. The tinkling sounds just like tiny bells ringing. The friends look up and say bells together.

The dotted line splits at a knot in the trunk and goes two ways. One branch goes left and points toward thick prickly leaves that look sharp and mean. The other branch goes right and points along a smooth low limb that looks easy to walk on. Not scary at all to see. The friends hear the bells ringing and wonder which way to go. The bells sound happiest on the right they think.

choices.label: Walk along the smooth low limb.

nodes.body: The prickly left branch is tangled and full of twigs and branches going nowhere good. It leads to an old bird box that is empty with no treasure and no X marked anywhere. The friends giggle at the silly dead end and hop off the branch. They head back to the porch because they know the X is hiding somewhere else.

The smooth limb is easy to walk on. Very easy. The tinkling gets clearer with every step. Ting ting ting. Louder now. Nina keeps one hand on the trunk. For safety. For balance. Theo watches the map. He points the way. The limb curves ahead. It curves toward a sunny gap. A gap in the green leaves.

They reach a special and magical place in the tree where hundreds of little things dangle. The things hang from twigs on bits of string in different lengths. Keys hang there and shells too, and spoons and buttons knock and clink together. They make a gentle sound that fills the air all around them. The whole tree seems to sing with soft music.

Someone made a wind chime long ago. On every single branch. Each chime is different and special. Sunlight bounces off the shiny bits. It scatters rainbows. Little rainbows. They dance on the green leaves. Nina and Theo stand very still. They just listen. The song is beautiful.

In the middle hangs the biggest chime. The grandest chime. It is made from an old watering can. And shining spoons. The map's red X floats right beside it. Right here. Nina reaches out slowly. Very slowly. She gives it the gentlest touch. Ting, the chime says softly. One small, sweet sound.

The friends have found a secret wind-chime tree that sings and makes music all by itself. It sings whenever the breeze blows and moves the hanging things. They add one small shell of their own to the branches so the tree will change. Now the tree will sing louder and sweeter and happier than before. They walk home humming and the song stays with them all the way.

The dotted line points across the lawn. To the little green shed. It sits near the fence. At the back of the garden. Theo hops over a colorful flowerbed. Over the flowers he goes. Nina reads the map. She holds it high. The shed door hangs open. Just a crack. A little opening. Something inside gives a soft creak. Creak, the door says. An old, friendly sound.

choices.label: Sneak up to the garden shed.

nodes.body: Behind the shed the trail splits into two paths that go different ways. One line slips inside the shed's open door into the cool shadows and darkness within. It is dark and cool inside that place. The other path runs off toward the rock pile. Big grey rocks are stacked high against the back fence. The friends pick which one to explore and follow.

choices.label: Head to the big rock pile.

nodes.body: They push the shed door open. Slowly. Carefully. Dusty light spills in. Golden dusty light. They see pots. Old flowerpots. And hoses. Coiled hoses. It smells like soil. Like old wood. A garden smell. The dotted line leads to the far dark corner. Behind the wheelbarrow.

choices.label: Follow the light to the corner.

nodes.body: Nina tiptoes past rakes and watering cans. Past the garden tools. Theo holds the map steady. Very steady. In the corner is a stack of crates. Wooden crates piled up. Like steps going up. Up and up. A faint light glows from a gap. Near the roof. Up high. Something up there looks cozy. Warm and cozy.

choices.label: Look at the stack of crates.

nodes.body: The map is torn right at this spot. Two ways go forward. A low gap leads under a workbench. Into darkness. Very dark. A wobbly ladder of crates climbs up. Up toward the glowing gap. Up toward the roof. The crates shake a little. Which way should they go? The friends decide. They choose their way.

The low gap under the bench is just cobwebs. And old pots. A sleepy woodlouse lives there. The friends back out. Sneezing. Laughing. Dust is everywhere. They dust the map off. Back to the porch they trot. They skip and run. Cold lemonade waits. Sweet and cold. Before they try another trail.

The crate ladder wobbles just a little bit but it is strong and holds them well. The friends climb toward the warm glowing gap that shines from high up. Up and up they go together as a team. Theo goes first and climbs higher than Nina. Then he gasps softly and says oh wow in surprise. Nina scrambles up right behind him as fast as she can. What did you see, she asks him with wonder.

Up in the loft is a hideaway. A cozy hideaway. Someone built it long ago. It is made from old soft cushions. A milk-crate table. A lantern hangs down. It runs on batteries. It glows. A hand-painted sign hangs above. It feels warm and secret here.

Inside are two soft seats. So soft. Good for sitting. A shelf holds adventure books. Stories and stories. A jar of shiny paper stars sits there. They glimmer and gleam. A blanket is folded in the corner. Folded neatly. As if waiting. The map's red X is right here. Right in this special place.

The painted sign reads Explorers Welcome. The letters are bright and friendly. Nina and Theo realize something. This is a secret fort. Kids built it long ago. Other kids like them. They left it ready. Ready for whoever found the map. Nina and Theo step inside. Like honored guests. Like special guests.

The friends have found a secret fort. Hidden in the top of the shed. All ready for new explorers. Ready and waiting. They write their names in a logbook. Their own names. They promise to come back. To take good care of it. This will be their headquarters. Their special place. Their own secret fort now.

The other path leads to big grey rocks that are stacked high and warm. The sun has warmed them so they feel hot to touch. They feel warm and solid. A few marigolds grow between the rocks in the cracks and crevices. Bright yellow flowers shine there. A dotted line points to a low flat stone. Near the bottom of the pile. There it is right below them.

choices.label: Look at the low flat stone.

nodes.body: Theo taps the flat stone with his finger and it sounds hollow like a drum inside. The sound tells him there is a space underneath somewhere. Nina checks the map and the red X sits below the rocks right here. Right below everything. A little ring of pebbles marks the spot and shows the way. This is it, they say together. This is the place we are looking for.

Two small arrows are scratched on the stone. Faint scratches. One arrow points left. To a gap between boulders. Mossy boulders. The other arrow points down. At loose sandy soil. Right beneath the stone. The friends choose where to search. Where to dig. Which way feels right.

The gap between the boulders has ants. So many ants. They march back and forth. Back and forth. Doing their work. No treasure here. No X. The friends say hello to the ants. Goodbye, ants, they say. They roll up the map. Back to the porch they go. For a cold lemonade break.

They lift the flat stone. Gently. And move it to the side. The sandy soil underneath is soft. So soft. Easy to brush away. Theo digs with a small garden trowel. Scrape scrape scrape. Nina cups her hands. She brushes the dirt. Something smooth appears. Something round. What is it, they wonder.

It is a small tin box. With a rusty latch. So old. It sits nestled in the sand. Just where the X promised. Exactly. It is small enough for two hands to lift. The friends wiggle it free. Very carefully. They blow off the dust. Poof poof. Dust flies everywhere.

The latch pops open. Click. A happy sound. Happy click. Inside is an old checkered cloth. Soft and worn. Under the cloth are marbles. Glass marbles. So many marbles. They catch the sunlight. Swirls of blue. Green. Gold. Nina gasps. Oh, she says. How beautiful.

There are cats-eyes. And swirlies. Swirled patterns inside. One big shooter is as clear as a raindrop. Crystal clear. Dozens of marbles saved long ago. By some other kid. A tiny note says share and enjoy. The friends read it. Again and again. Share and enjoy, they whisper.

The friends have dug up a treasure box. Of beautiful glass marbles. Left by a kid from long long ago. They choose two favorites to keep. To remember. The rest they tuck back in the sand. With a note of their own. A note for the next explorer. The next lucky finder. They walk home with one marble in each pocket.

The last part of the line runs down. Down the sloping lawn. To the little stream at the bottom. The very bottom. The stream meets the park beyond. Water chatters over smooth stones. Splash splash splash. Happy water sounds. Theo holds the map up high. Very high. So it will not get wet.

choices.label: Head down to the little stream.

nodes.body: Down by the water the line splits one final time. The last split. One branch curves into tall blackberry bushes. Along the bank. Heavy with berries. Ripe purple berries. Dark and sweet looking. The other branch hops across flat stepping stones. To the far side of the stream. The friends decide which way. Where does the treasure lie.

choices.label: Follow the line into the brambles.

nodes.body: They follow the line into the bushes. Into the shade. Cool shade. Tall blackberry bushes surround them. Heavy with ripe purple berries. The leaves make a cool green tunnel. Like a green hall. From somewhere deep inside comes a soft meow. A friendly meow. It sounds warm and nice. Like someone is waiting.

choices.label: Follow the meow into the bushes.

nodes.body: Nina holds a branch aside. Carefully. So no thorns catch them. No scratches. The meow comes again. Closer this time. Much closer. Theo checks the map. The dotted line goes right in. Into the middle. The friends step in gently. Very gently. Soft steps. Quiet steps.

choices.label: Hold the thorns aside and go in.

nodes.body: Inside the bushes the trail is faded. Hard to see. Two little tunnels lead on. Through the leaves. Through the branches. One tunnel goes low. Toward a dark thorny thicket. The other goes toward bright sunlight. Warm sunshine. The meow sounds happiest there. In the warm light. They pick their way. Which tunnel calls to them.

The low tunnel ends in thick thorns. Too prickly to pass. No way through. A lost sock hangs on a branch. Just a sock. The friends back out. Very carefully. Not a single scratch. They did it. They laugh about the silly sock. Up to the porch they go. For a cool glass of lemonade.

The sunny tunnel opens up. Into a cozy clearing. A small clearing. Right in the heart of the brambles. The ground has soft dry leaves. Brown and crumbly. Nice smelling. The meow turns into a purr. A rumbly purr. Two green eyes blink at them. From a warm nest of grass.

A fluffy grey cat sits there. With white paws. So soft looking. It does not run away. Not at all. It stays. It stretches. It yawns big. It pads right over. To say hello. A little bell hangs on its neck. It jingles softly. Jingle jingle. A friendly sound.

choices.label: Say hello to the fluffy cat.

nodes.body: The cat winds around their ankles. Around and around. It purrs like a tiny motor. Purrrr. A warm sound. It flops over on its side. For a belly rub. The friends laugh and stroke its soft fur. So soft. So warm. The map's red X is right here. Right where the cat sits.

choices.label: Give the cat a belly rub.

nodes.body: The collar has a small tag. It reads Pepper, please be my friend. No address. No home written down. The cat clearly knows this bramble clearing well. Very well. It treats the friends like old pals. Like friends it has known. Nina and Theo wonder. Has Pepper been waiting? Waiting for the map?

The friends have found a cat named Pepper. Living here. In the brambles. Living happy and free. In a secret spot. They share their snack with him. Their afternoon snack. They promise to visit every day. Every single day. To help find where he belongs. Pepper walks them halfway home. His tail held high.

The stepping stones cross the stream. In a neat flat row. They are dry on top. Easy to hop from one to the next. The friends cross one careful stone at a time. One stone. Then another. To the mossy far bank. The dotted line points to a willow tree. A leaning willow tree.

choices.label: Hop across to the far bank.

nodes.body: On the far bank the willow trails long green fingers. In the water. Dripping wet. The ground beneath the roots is soft. And cool. Very cool. Theo studies the map. He holds it carefully. The red X sits. Tucked among the tangled roots. Something glints. Shines. Faintly glinting in the shade. In the cool shade.

Two hollows show among the roots. Two little holes. One is a wet muddy dip. Right at the water's edge. Very muddy. Very wet. The other is a dry spot. A sheltered nook higher up. The glinting comes from there. From the dry spot. The friends choose where to search.

The muddy dip holds a shiny wet pebble. Just a pebble. And one very surprised water beetle. It looks shocked. Shocked to see them. The friends get mud on their shoes. On their feet. They grin at the muddy mess. They laugh. They wave to the beetle. Goodbye, beetle, they say. Back across the stones they hop.

The dry nook is just right. Just the right size to kneel in. To crouch down. The glinting comes from something half-buried. In the cool soil. Nina brushes the earth away. Very gently. Slowly. Theo holds the willow branches back. So they do not get in the way. A corner of metal appears. Metal gleams.

It is a small brass tin. Green at the edges. Old and green. Exactly where the map's X promised. Right here. The friends work the stiff lid. With careful fingers. Patient fingers. Slow and steady. At last it opens. A soft puff of old air. Whoooosh.

Inside the tin are shiny buttons. So many buttons. A whole heap. On a bed of faded velvet. Soft velvet. Brass buttons. Glass buttons. Pearly buttons shaped like flowers. Little flower buttons. They sparkle like jewels. Like real jewels. In the dappled light. Dappled green light.

Someone kept these buttons safe. For many many years. Each button is different. And lovely. And unique. Each one special. A true backyard treasure. A folded slip of paper says for the finder, with love. With love, the friends whisper. They feel like they have opened a chest. A pirate's chest of gold.

The friends have unearthed a hidden tin. Of dazzling shiny buttons. The real treasure. At the end of the map. They pick one gleaming button each. To keep. As memories. They rebury the tin with a note. A new note. For the next explorer. The next lucky finder. They cross the stepping stones home. Feeling rich as kings.
